blueprint_refs: OUTPUT FORMAT STRICT: serialize each simulated day as a single string exactly matching the 1921Y.json day-record format

evidence.metrics: execution_status=success (no crash), but large unrelated subsystem exists in CURRENT CODE

offspec-review-llm-subsystem-present.error_identification: Codebase contains a review-writing LLM subsystem unrelated to mobility simulation, increasing dependency and failure surface without contributing to required outputs.
offspec-review-llm-subsystem-present.root_cause_analysis: CURRENT CODE includes OpenAI/Responses API calls and review authoring agents that are not referenced by the mobility pipeline but violate scope and can break runs when OPENAI_API_KEY/openai package is missing (even if not triggered). Location: call_gpt5_with_responses_api + ReasoningAgent stack.
offspec-review-llm-subsystem-present.correct_approach: Remove the entire review/LLM subsystem (OpenAI import, get_openai_api_key, call_gpt5_with_responses_api, MemoryAgent/PlanningAgent/ReasoningAgent/ReviewAuthor) unless explicitly required for mobility Persona inference. If Persona inference is desired later, implement it behind a feature flag and ensure default path is offline and deterministic.
offspec-review-llm-subsystem-present.key_insight: Keep generated simulators single-purpose: delete unused subsystems that introduce external dependencies, even if they are not executed in the main path.

home-insertion-inflates-stop-count-error.error_identification: Daily stop counts are systematically mismatched (mean error ~0.79 stops/day; KL ~0.39), consistent with the simulator adding extra visits beyond the sampled stop budget.
home-insertion-inflates-stop-count-error.root_cause_analysis: generate_day samples stop_count, then may append an extra late-day return-home visit without accounting for the stop budget (it does not decrement/integrate into stop_count). This shifts the simulated stop-count distribution upward and distorts KL. Location: generate_day post-loop 'return home' append.
home-insertion-inflates-stop-count-error.correct_approach: Make stop budget binding: either (a) treat home-return as one of the planned stops by reserving capacity (e.g., simulate stop_count-1 non-home stops then optionally add home as last stop), or (b) include the home-return hazard inside the step loop as a termination/next-action choice so the realized length matches the modeled distribution. Refit/retune return-home bias after enforcing budget.
home-insertion-inflates-stop-count-error.key_insight: When a metric evaluates an output count, any post-hoc appends must be counted in the same budget; never add events after sampling a fixed count without compensating logic.

evidence.metrics: trip_distance_wasserstein=2.554704637987986; gt coord_match_rate=0.3010 vs sim coord_match_rate=0.2748 (validation meta)

trip-distance-mismatch-from-default-dist.error_identification: Trip distance distribution is notably mismatched (Wasserstein ~2.55 km units) while only ~27–30% of consecutive pairs have coordinates, meaning the metric is computed on a sparse, biased subset.
trip-distance-mismatch-from-default-dist.root_cause_analysis: When coords are missing, the simulator uses a fixed default_km=3.0 for distance penalties and travel-time proxy, making location choice and timing weakly coupled to geography for many moves. Additionally, candidates_for_category mixes 'nearest by anchor coords' with random fill of missing-coord POIs, creating a simulated coordinate-availability subset different from ground truth (coord_match_rate differs). The evaluated distance sample is thus biased and the control signal for calibration is noisy.
trip-distance-mismatch-from-default-dist.correct_approach: Improve distance handling under missing coords: (1) in _choose_next_poi, avoid applying distance penalties based on arbitrary default_km; instead down-weight distance terms when either endpoint lacks coords (e.g., treat as unknown and rely on preference/transition), or impute distances via category-level typical distance distributions learned from coord-available transitions. (2) In candidates_for_category, match ground-truth coordinate availability by sampling coord-known vs coord-unknown POIs in proportions closer to observed for that category/user. (3) In calibration/evaluation logs, report trip-distance metric together with coord_match_rate and matched_pairs counts and optionally optimize a composite that penalizes coord_match_rate drift.
trip-distance-mismatch-from-default-dist.key_insight: Distance metrics are only meaningful when the coord-available subset is representative; if coords are sparse, either impute distances from learned distributions or explicitly control/monitor coordinate-availability bias.

blueprint_refs: "Initialize at sampled start time; ... ensuring monotonic increase within day"

evidence.metrics: tod_jsd_avg=0.03148900009153471 (good overall), but sample outputs include very early 00:xx starts (e.g., 2021-09-23 Home#582 at 00:20:00)

start-time-and-tod-model-misses-overnigh.error_identification: Although ToD JSD is decent on average, simulated days sometimes start at extreme times (00:xx) and produce long chains across the night, suggesting the model may be conflating late-night activity with next-day starts.
start-time-and-tod-model-misses-overnigh.root_cause_analysis: The parser clamps non-monotone timestamps within the listed order (parse_day_record), but does not model cross-midnight wrap; any records that implicitly cross midnight can be coerced into non-decreasing times within the same day. This can inflate start_time_hist mass near 0 and push generated start times earlier than realistic for some users.
start-time-and-tod-model-misses-overnigh.correct_approach: Detect and handle overnight wrap during parsing: when a time decreases relative to previous within a day string, interpret it as next-day continuation and either (a) drop wrapped visits from that day record, or (b) cap at 23:59 and stop parsing further visits, or (c) split into two day records if the dataset semantics allow (blueprint implies per-day records; safest is to stop parsing at first wrap). Recompute start_time_hist after this fix.
start-time-and-tod-model-misses-overnigh.key_insight: For per-day trajectory models, never force non-monotone within-day times to be monotone by clamping; explicitly treat decreases as an overnight boundary event.

category-transition-model-ignores-coarse.error_identification: Transitions and category shares are already strong, but the implementation is partially misaligned with the blueprint’s coarse-category and exogenous modulation intent, limiting headroom and robustness.
category-transition-model-ignores-coarse.root_cause_analysis: Training transitions are built on fine categories only (fit_pattern_from_training uses v.category), while evaluation transitions are computed on coarse-mapped categories (Evaluator._collect_transitions uses to_coarse_category). Additionally, exogenous signals are only used for a narrow weekend office down-weight heuristic; month/seasonality is unused, and day-of-week does not modulate stop counts or time profiles as specified.
category-transition-model-ignores-coarse.correct_approach: Align modeling space: either (a) learn transitions on coarse categories (apply to_coarse_category during pattern fitting) to match evaluation and blueprint, or (b) evaluate on fine categories consistently (but blueprint prefers coarse mapping when available). Extend exogenous conditioning: adjust time_of_day_category_profile and/or mixture weights by day_of_week and month (global learned multipliers from training), and optionally modulate stop_multiplier by weekend/weekday.
category-transition-model-ignores-coarse.key_insight: Train/evaluate in the same categorical space; if evaluation uses coarse mapping, learn transitions/preferences in coarse space too, then layer fine POI choice underneath.
